title: Loss Aversion
author: From: Thinking, Fast and Slow
content: The pain of losing is psychologically about twice as powerful as the joy of gaining. This leads to irrational risk-avoidance.
action: Action: List one 'safe' decision you are making only to avoid loss, and re-evaluate it.

title: The Anchoring Effect
author: From: Predictably Irrational
content: Your first piece of information sets the 'anchor' for all subsequent judgments. Be wary of the first number you see in a negotiation.
action: Action: When researching a price or timeline, gather three sources before settling on a mental anchor.

title: Confirmation Bias
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: You subconsciously filter out information that contradicts your existing beliefs. The antidote is seeking disconfirming evidence.
action: Action: Actively look for one argument that proves your current main project hypothesis wrong.

title: The Availability Heuristic
author: From: Thinking, Fast and Slow
content: We judge the probability of an event by how easily examples come to mind. This is why we fear rare events and ignore common ones.
action: Action: Differentiate between a 'memorable' risk and a 'probable' risk in your workflow.

title: Choice Architecture
author: From: Nudge
content: The way choices are presented significantly impacts the decision made. Design your environment to make good choices easier.
action: Action: Simplify your environment to reduce 'decision fatigue' during your workday.
